reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — verified-runnable-first (real headless-Chrome inference on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build against a local mirror of the identical HF files), then driven headless-Chrome page matrix (desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, light+dark screenshots read)

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable BEFORE building: loaded FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-italian-onnx via AutoModelForCTC on the transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build (import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@huggingface/transformers@3.7.5")) in real headless Chrome (WASM EP), fed the Public-Domain Carlo Collodi 'Le avventure di Pinocchio' clip through the exact worker code path (architecture config borrowed from the base jonatasgrosman repo + subfolder:"" + dtype fp32 + manual wav2vec2 zero-mean/unit-variance normalisation + hand-built 44-symbol CTC decode) and got REAL logits dims [1, 599, 44] and a coherent Italian transcript: 'un bambino c'era una volta un re diranno subito i miei piccoli lettori no ragazzi avete sbagliato c'era una volta' — the iconic opening of Pinocchio (~10.8s decode on WASM). A second clip returned 'ma il fatto gli è che un bel giorno questo pezzo di legno capitò nella bottega di un vecchio falegname il quale aveva nome mastrantonio ...' — also coherent Italian. Source loads the model in a Web Worker (models/italian-xlsr-asr/worker.js) and does a real greedy CTC argmax→collapse — no canned result.
rubric.notes: The files were served from a LOCAL mirror of the identical HF repo (model.onnx = 1262336608 bytes, downloaded whole from the HF CDN via curl) so the 1.2 GB fp32 load could complete inside the harness time budget; the code path, config, feature extraction and decode are byte-identical to production (which fetches the same files from the HF CDN). The candidate FinDIT repo ships ONLY model.onnx (single self-contained fp32 file, ~1.26 GB, no external-data sidecar) + tokenizer.json, so no config.json/preprocessor_config.json — handled + disclosed.

rubric.evidence: Output is an Italian transcript string whose words match the spoken audio (verified against the Public-Domain LibriVox 'Le avventure di Pinocchio' source — ch.1, the famous 'C'era una volta...' opening). Real logits [1, 599, 44] over the 44-symbol Latin-Italian vocab. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-word forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × 44-symbol vocab → collapsed text + per-word timings) is correct; Italian elisions (c'era) and accents appear in real output.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (FinDIT-Studio/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-italian-onnx linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (fp32), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the 44-symbol Latin-Italian vocab (à á è é ì í ò ó ù ú + š), dtype fp32, AND honestly explains the three non-standard load quirks: the single fp32 model.onnx at the repo root (loaded with subfolder:""), the missing config.json (architecture config borrowed from the base jonatasgrosman repo), and the missing preprocessor_config.json (feature normalisation done in-worker).
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype fp32, subfolder "", config from base repo). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the ~1.2 GB one-time download and the base-model Apache-2.0 license (the ONNX-export repo declares none).

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh profile on all 5 pages: loader in 'download-required' with a single honest 'Download model (~1200 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified loaderBtns == ['Download model (~1200 MB)'] on main/basics/practical/wild; multi-model shows the two honest buttons '~1200 MB' + '~120 MB'). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (backed by the version-agnostic model-cache), which auto-inits a cached-current version with only a Clear-cache button — the same lib verified on the other built demos.
rubric.notes: Cold absent-state + no-silent-download driven directly. The warm cached auto-init path was not separately driven on the CDN in this pass because the 1.2 GB fp32 download does not complete within the headless harness time budget in this environment (openQuestion) — the model load itself is proven via the local-mirror run; the auto-init behaviour is the shared loader's, exercised by the rest of the catalogue.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM. Driven pages: 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across all 5.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, Italian sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-word chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (Italian) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Driven check: 0 unnamed buttons on every page. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile (download-required). The Italian transcript that appears is produced by the on-device CTC run (logits [1,599,44] observed).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Italian-phonetic approximation) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Italian regions marked lang="it"; per-word replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix on all 5 pages: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch) — measured desktopOverflow=0 and mobileOverflow=0 on every page. audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). Light + dark desktop + dark mobile screenshots read and confirmed on-brand (warm paper / indigo, Georgia display; dark mode legible).
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Driven pages stayed responsive with a clean console.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow, wide code blocks scroll internally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing and flexible tracks before fixed width/height; use logical properties (inline-size/block-size/max-inline-size); use flex-wrap+gap so items reflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls (design-system pre overflow). Verified desktopOverflow=0 and mobileOverflow=0 at 360×740 and 1280×800 across all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0/0 on every page

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live async loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; give every control an accessible name; keep buttons keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-word replay buttons carry aria-labels; Italian content marked lang="it" for correct SR pronunciation. Driven: 0 unnamed buttons across all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/italian-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible file upload and microphone capture button
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use real labelled controls; a hidden <input type=file> triggered by a visible button; honest handling of denied/unavailable capture.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Upload is a visible <button> that clicks a hidden accessible file input; the mic uses getUserMedia with a visible record toggle and an explicit no-microphone fallback panel pointing to sample/upload (user-initiated capture only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: MicRecorder.supported() gate + micFallback panel; upload wired to file input; driven sample path produced real output

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Drive the full production CDN path end-to-end in a headless run (Download the 1.2 GB fp32 over the HF CDN → ready → Transcribe) — the local-mirror run proved the model/features/decode, but the in-harness CDN download exceeds the time budget in this environment; a patient run is queued.
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-word replay buttons announce and operate correctly?

Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run) — matters more for the ~1.2 GB fp32 download.
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (expect faster decode with identical output).

followUpGoals.goal: Add a mobile memory/storage pre-check before the ~1.2 GB fp32 download on constrained devices (this model has no quantized export, so the transfer is unusually large).
Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for italian-xlsr-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the transcript and per-frame grid).
If a genuine q8/q4 Italian XLSR ONNX with a real CTC head (and a config.json) ever ships, add a lighter variant as a NEW slug so the demo isn't a 1.2 GB download — do not repurpose this fp32 page.

summary: italian-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Italian — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is the 44-symbol Latin-Italian set (à á è é ì í ò ó ù ú + š) — materially different from the built English wav2vec2, Russian XLSR (Cyrillic), French XLSR, Spanish XLSR and Portuguese XLSR (different language specialisations). Verified runnable on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build BEFORE building: real logits [1,599,44] and a coherent Italian transcript (the iconic 'C'era una volta...' opening of Pinocchio) on the Public-Domain sample. The FinDIT ONNX-export repo ships only a single self-contained fp32 model.onnx (~1.26 GB, no external-data sidecar) + tokenizer.json — no config.json/preprocessor_config.json — so the worker borrows the architecture config from the base jonatasgrosman repo, normalises audio itself, loads with subfolder:"" + dtype fp32, and CTC-decodes from the 44-symbol vocab; all disclosed at-a-glance. Driven headless-Chrome matrix on all 5 pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model): fresh profile shows a labelled ~1.2 GB Download with no silent transfer, 0 horizontal overflow desktop+mobile, 0 console errors, 0 failed requests, 0 unnamed buttons; light+dark screenshots read. dtype fp32 chosen honestly (no q8/q4 Italian XLSR ONNX export exists). Multi-model translation uses Xenova/opus-mt-it-en (verified runnable pt/it→en). Remaining work: a patient full-CDN end-to-end run, a keyboard/SR traversal, and explicit partial/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
